question: Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market and how AI is affecting various industries.

evidence_for_gen.title: Technological Innovation | World Economic Forum
evidence_for_gen.query_origin: impact of AI on the labor market fourth industrial revolution
evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:
# Technological Innovation | World Economic Forum
We use cookies to improve your experience. Accept all cookies to continue.
* [Consent](https://www.weforum.org/#) [Details](https://www.weforum.org/#)
Artificial intelligence is a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, reshaping how work is organized across manufacturing, services, and knowledge industries.
Automation of routine cognitive tasks has displaced some clerical roles while creating demand for data and oversight roles.
Subscribe to our newsletter for the latest World Economic Forum reports.
Studies of firm-level adoption find that AI complements high-skill labor and substitutes for a subset of mid-skill routine tasks.

evidence_for_gen.title: Automation and New Tasks: How Technology Displaces and Reinstates Labor
evidence_for_gen.query_origin: automation task displacement reinstatement labor demand
evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:
We present a framework for understanding the effects of automation on labor demand and use it to interpret changes in US employment.
At the center of our framework is the allocation of tasks to capital and labor and how new technologies change that allocation.
Automation displaces labor from tasks it previously performed, exerting downward pressure on wages and employment.
The reinstatement effect creates new labor-intensive tasks that raise the demand for labor and can offset displacement.
evidence_for_gen.statement: Automation and New Tasks: How Technology Displaces and Reinstates Labor

evidence_for_gen.title: Spinal cord stimulation for chronic pain: a systematic review
evidence_for_gen.query_origin: spinal cord stimulation chronic pain outcomes
evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:
Spinal cord stimulation is a neuromodulation therapy used for chronic neuropathic pain.
The systematic review pooled twelve randomized trials of dorsal column stimulation.
Pain relief at twelve months favored stimulation over conventional medical management.
evidence_for_gen.statement: Spinal cord stimulation for chronic pain: a systematic review

evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:

evidence_for_gen.title: Early evidence on AI and employment, 2019
evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:
This 2019 study examines early firm adoption of machine learning and its association with employment growth.
Firms adopting AI reported reallocation of workers toward non-routine analytical tasks.
The results are descriptive and predate large-language-model diffusion.
evidence_for_gen.statement: Early evidence on AI and employment, 2019

evidence_for_gen.title: AI and labor demand: a conceptual synthesis
evidence_for_gen.direct_quote:
This synthesis integrates task-based models of AI and labor demand across industries.
It argues that the net employment effect depends on the balance of displacement and reinstatement.
Industry heterogeneity shapes which occupations are most exposed to automation.
evidence_for_gen.statement: AI and labor demand: a conceptual synthesis
